krama: नमः॑ श्र॒वाय॑ । श्र॒वाय॑ च । च प्रतिश्र॒वाय॑ । प्रतिश्र॒वाय॑ च । च इति॑ च ॥

dhyana.english: One should meditate on the Lord in the form of Ardhanarishvara (half man, half woman), seated on a chariot, holding an arrow in his hand, and holding a bow (the golden Meru mountain) placed there by the lotus hands of Gauri.

bhatta_bhaskara.english:
'Namah shravaya cha'—to him in the form of sound. It is called 'shravah' because it is heard. Or: to him who is heard everywhere, the very famous. [Grammatical note]. 'Pratishravaya cha'—to him in the form of an echo, or in the form of a reply (response). (10)

Salutation to the one with a swift army and to the one with a swift chariot.

dhyana.english: One should meditate on the Lord in the form of Ardhanarishvara (half man, half woman), seated on a chariot, holding an arrow in his hand, and holding a bow (the golden Meru mountain) placed there by the lotus hands of Gauri.

abhinava_shankara.sanskrit:
षष्ठोऽनुवाकः ॥

                         ———

      नमो ज्येष्ठाय च कनिष्ठाय च नमः
      पूर्वजाय चापरजाय च नमो मध्यमाय
      चापगल्भाय च नमो जघन्याय च बुध्ि-
      याय च नमः सोभ्याय च प्रतिसर्याय
      च नमो याम्याय च क्षेमन्याय च नम-
      उर्वर्याय च खल्याय च नमः श्रोकयाय
      चावसान्याय च नमो वन्याय च कक्ष्याय
      च नमः श्रवाय च प्रतिश्रवाय च नम
      आशुषेणाय चाशुरथाय च नमः शूराय
      चाविभिन्दते च नमो धर्मिणे च वर्रूथिने
      च नमो बिल्मिने च कवचिने च नमः
      श्रुताय च श्रुतसेनाय च ॥

      पञ्चमे विविधमहिह्मोपेतत्वेन भगवान्नमस्कृतः, षष्ठे तु
सर्वात्मकत्वसर्वान्तयोमित्वाभ्यां नमस्किर्यते—— नमो ज्येष्ठे-
abhinava_shankara.english:
Sixth Anuvāka (ṣaṣṭho'nuvākaḥ)

                         ———

      Homage to Jyeṣṭha and to Kaniṣṭha; homage
      to Pūrvaja and to Aparaja; homage to Madhyama
      and to Apagalbha; homage to Jaghanya and to Budhnya;
      homage to Sobhya and to Pratisarya;
      homage to Yāmya and to Kṣemya; homage
      to Urvaryā and to Khalya; homage to Śrokya
      and to Avasānya; homage to Vanya and to Kakṣya;
      homage to Śrava and to Pratiśrava; homage
      to Āśuṣeṇa and to Āśuratha; homage to Śūra
      and to Avabhindat; homage to Dharmiṇe and to Varūthine;
      homage to Bilmine and to Kavacine; homage
      to Śruta and to Śrutasena.

      In the fifth anuvāka, the Lord was saluted as the possessor of diverse glories; in the sixth, He is saluted by virtue of His being the Soul of all (sarvātmakatva) and His inner presence in all things (sarvāntaryāmitva) — "Namo jyeṣṭhā-
